raw.Name: learning_rate_schedule
raw.Title: Adaptive Learning Rate Schedules: Comparing different learning rate schedules for diffusion models.
raw.Experiment: In this experiment, we compare the performance of different learning rate schedules on diffusion model performance. We use the final estimated KL as the evaluation metric.

rationale: Captured from AI-Scientist v1 ideas.json. Patch v1 logging to capture queries.

raw.Name: self_conditioned_mode_control
raw.Title: Discovering Controllable Modes in Low-Dimensional Diffusion via Self-Supervised Pseudo-Labels
raw.Experiment: Add a preprocessing step that assigns pseudo-labels to each dataset point using a simple in-code k-means clustering on the 2D coordinates. Use a small ablation over the number of clusters, e.g. K in {2,4}, to test how granularity affects controllability. Modify MLPDenoiser to include a label embedding nn.Embedding(K+1, embedding_dim), where the extra index is a null condition for classifier-free guidance. During training, concatenate the label embedding with the existing x1, x2, and t embeddings, and randomly replace the true pseudo-label with the null label with probability 0.1 to 0.2. Train with the same DDPM noise-prediction loss. During sampling, generate unconditional samples and conditional samples for each pseudo-mode, with an optional small sweep of guidance weights such as {0,1,2,4}. Evaluate three main metrics: (1) global KL divergence between all generated samples and real data, (2) conditional KL divergence between samples generated for pseudo-mode k and the subset of real data assigned to pseudo-mode k, and (3) condition-consistency accuracy by assigning each generated sample to the nearest training k-means centroid and checking whether it matches the requested mode. Compare against the original unconditional DDPM to measure the tradeoff between controllability and overall sample quality.
